title: Let's Drink – Liqueur, the Sweet Temptation

seo.title: Let's Drink - Liqueur, the Sweet Temptation - Marinda Kook

html:
One can't live on food alone. And once you've eaten well, there are few things that come close to a little liqueur. The liqueur I'm making here is a base for other liqueurs. Because it has a cream base it must be kept in the fridge. It can keep in the fridge for up to 2 months – mine has never lasted that long.

 

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 1 tin condensed milk
  • 250 ml cream
  • 350 ml whisky – less if you prefer it not so strong
  • 5 ml vanilla extract
  • 5ml caramel extract

NOW:

  1. pour the condensed milk into a bowl
  2. add the cream
  3. pour in your whisky
  4. then add your vanilla and caramel extract
  5. put it in a food processor or whisk it together very well

I filled the bottles with boiling water and left them standing for a few minutes to sterilise them.

The first bottle I kept just as it was. Into the second bottle I stirred strong coffee (80ml) to make a coffee liqueur. It's a lovely Christmas gift these days.

Put your feet up on the coffee table and then I just say “Cheers”.
